text: Summit belt bag carried my life through a music festival and a 10k in the same weekend.
Eight days to refund my Latitude jacket. Within their stated window, outside my patience. The jacket I swapped to is great, for what it is worth.
Bought two sizes of the Form bra to try, returned one, standard practice everywhere. Relay flagged my account for high return rate after literally one return. The email was insulting.
The label they emailed expired before I could use it because I was traveling. Getting a new label issued took two support contacts and four days. Labels should not expire in two weeks.
fleece zipper is chunky and satisfying. pockets are deep. what more do you want.
i did the math and i've bought 31 relay items. all 31 still in use. unheard of
My December order arrived in mid January. No expedited reshipping offered, no credit, just a sorry for the delay template. After eight years of loyalty that template hurt more than the delay.
my daughter saved up for the checkpoint legging in december. backordered. it arrived in late january after the gift moment had long passed. great pants, terrible memory.
the checkpoint waistband pocket fits a hotel key card, a lip balm, and a $20. that's a whole night out in one waistband just saying

App store says the latest update fixed stability issues. It also relocated my order history, reset my notification preferences, and forgot my size profile. Stable, sure. Stable like a horse that bites.
Tracking stuck on arrived at facility for 6 days. Chat support copy-pasted the same answer twice. I could have run the package here from Boulder faster.
Sized up per the reviews and the Momentum Short still fit tight in the thigh. The fabric is beautiful. The fit chart is fiction.
Wanted to swap a size. Spent 40 minutes between the returns portal and chat to learn that swapping doesn't exist, only returning and rebuying. In 2025? Really?
I'm a mail sorter on overnight shifts and the Daily Tee keeps up with 8 hours of lifting and reaching in a warehouse that can't decide on a temperature. Five shirts deep now.
The Momentum Short has pockets deep enough that my phone has never once ejected during box jumps. My old shorts launched a phone into a stranger's squat rack. Never again. Never forget. lol
Promo code box disappears on mobile checkout when the keyboard opens. Typed my code blind, got it wrong twice, gave up and paid full price.
honestly Transit Jogger waistband is the most comfortable thing to ever touch my body. Sorry to my cat.
Flow tank, lovely. Arrived with a tiny pull in the fabric. Kept it because returning was more hassle than it was worth. Three stars.
Bought Stride Socks for running, now my whole sock drawer is Relay. It happened gradually then suddenly.
momentum short for ocr racing. barbed wire crawls, rope climbs, fire jumps. two seasons. one pair. zero repairs. they should sponsor the race series at this point.
stride socks turned my marathon buddy into a relay person. the pipeline is real
two months in: Every fall I think about buying a different brand fleece. Every fall I buy another Basecamp.
The Form bra band runs tight and the cups run generous. Together that's a fit nobody has. Sized around it eventually but the chart was zero help.
Three years of CrossFit in the same two pairs of Checkpoints. Rope climbs included. ROPE CLIMBS. Not even a pulled thread.
Form bra survived a year of hot yoga. The elastic finally relaxed, which is fair. Rebuy price went up fifteen percent, which is less fair.
Form bra has a tag that doesn't itch. Then I realized it's printed, not sewn. Then I realized someone thought about my comfort at the millimeter level. Loyal forever.
My Checkpoints get complimented at least once a week. I've started keeping a tally. We're at 47.
Every fall I think about buying a different brand fleece. Every fall I buy another Basecamp. 10/10
checkpoints have made every other legging in my drawer obsolete. they sit there. unworn. a museum of my former bad decisions. the checkpoints reign alone
The map on the tracking page literally showed my package going in a circle. Ten days of geometry.
Good shorts, good fleece, good socks. Nothing wrong, nothing exciting. Relay is reliable and that is worth three stars on autopilot.
I tried to find something wrong with the Momentum Short for this review. I could not. The liner is soft, the pockets work, the length is right. Annoyingly perfect.
Daily Tee: the fabric has a weight to it that cheap tees don't. You can feel the difference in the first five seconds. You can see it after the fiftieth wash.
Three weeks. THREE. A warehouse error, then a carrier error, then a reshipment. Each step I had to chase them. Nobody ever reached out first.
daily tee: owned 3 weeks, worn 17 times. the math is mathing
Basecamp Fleece in the heather gray is the softest thing Relay has ever made.
After years here my honest advice: buy core lines true to size, size up in Flow, size down in Momentum, and never ever trust the quiz. The fact that I NEED this advice is the review.
The map on the tracking page literally showed my package going in a circle. Ten days of geometry.
A week to get here but everything was right. Once upon a time it was two days AND everything was right. Aim higher, you've done it before.
My Summit duffel is four years old and looks better than bags I bought last year. Patina, baby. lol
transit jogger cuffs bunch over my sneakers more than my old pair did. tiny pattern change somewhere. still my go-to travel pant.
Relay is the only brand where I buy without reading reviews first. They've earned it.
Still the best activewear quality I have found, period. Just bracing you, new buyers, shipping is four days and your inbox will never be the same. ymmv
skort gang. that's it that's the review
App works fine 28 days a month. The two days that matter, drop days, it falls over. Sort of like a weather app that only breaks during storms.
Form bra plus Flow legging is the combo. Both excellent. Both shipped in separate boxes three days apart for reasons nobody explained. Wasteful and weird, but the gear is great.
Checkpoint Knee Legging review from a skeptic: I rolled my eyes at the price, my sister bought them for me anyway, and I have since purchased two more pairs with my own money. The end.
Court Skort has pockets deep enough for a phone AND tennis balls. Game changer.
No front seam on the Checkpoint. Whoever decided that, thank you. THANK YOU.
Search results show sold out items first. Page after page of things I cannot buy before a single available product. Sort by actually purchasable should be the default, no?

PSA: Honestly the return was fine, a week-ish. The annoying part was repackaging since they want original tags attached and the tags are attached by plastic loops you cut to try things on. Think it through.
PSA: I coach high school track and stand in the sun for hours. The Daily Tee doesn't trap heat, doesn't show sweat patches badly, and survives industrial laundering. A+.
The Summit neck gaiter is the winter running MVP. Breathes when you need it to.
update: Ordered a large based on the chart measurements I took with an actual tape measure. Fit like a medium. Either the chart is wrong or my tape measure is gaslighting me.
quick note - Two of my last three orders arrived later than the promised window. The gear is great when it shows up. When is the operative word.
used the predictor, got the wrong size, returned it, refund took forever, rebought, price had jumped, emailed support, got a coupon that excluded the item i was buying. every single step failed me.
My Basecamp Fleece has been to two national parks and a hundred school dropoffs. Holding strong.
Visited the Boulder clubhouse on vacation like it was a national monument. Bought a fleece. No regrets.
Got logged out while writing a product review in the app. Lost the review. Writing this one on my laptop out of spite. The Transit Joggers are great, the app is not.
pockets. finally.
two months in: still love this brand but the 3 to 4 day standard quietly became a week and nobody said anything. at least be honest about the new timeline at checkout.
the momentum short looks tailored. shorts. tailored. my legs have never been so flattered

fwiw eight years buying relay and the december legging sellout was the first time i felt like a number. backordered, no timeline, generic updates. the product that finally arrived in january is admittedly perfect.
Size 1X per their own chart measurements. Predictor said XL. XL didn't fti. Their chart and their tool aren't even on speaking terms.
App search cannot exclude sold out items but the homepage can show me 40 personalized picks instantly. Engineering effort is going somewhere, just not where I need it.
ngl form sports bra. four years of owning sports bras from six brands. this is the one. it was always the one. don't waste years like i did
quick note - the high collar on the basecamp is doing the lord's work on windy days
The Basecamp Fleece pockets are lined with something soft. I noticed. I appreciated.
rained the entire relay day 5k last month. my latitude and i finished happy
the jogger drawstring never disappears into the waistband. relay thought of everything
Checkpoints at a week-long yoga retreat. Two pairs, fourteen classes, one very smug me when everyone else's leggings went sheer in candlelight flow.
Did a 90 minute vinyasa in the Flow set. Zero adjusting. That never happens.
quick note - five stars for the summit belt bag. race day, dog walks, farmers market. the everything bag.
Wore the Form bra for a 6am lift, kept it on through a 10 hour clinic day, forgot about it entirely. Comfortable enough to live in, supportive enough to train in.
Checkpoints get a strong four. The fabric, fit, and pocket are five sta.r The wait when my size restocked after the holiday rush was not. Still my favorite leggings ever owned.
Latitude shell over the Form bra and Flow leggings. Rain run complete. All Relay, all dry.
the form bra doesn't have that weird boob-shelf uniboob thing. shaped like an actual human. 5 stars 10/10
Ordered a birthday gift 12 days out. Should have been easy. It came two days after the party. The tracking sat on pre-shipment for almost a week and support could not explain why.
Seven days, and the box arrived looking like it had been kicked across three states. Tee was fine but come on.
Trail runner. The Checkpoint protects my knees from brush without overheating my legs. The exact legging length nobody else makes well. Relay nailed it.
4 stars only because they should make a dress sock version. I'd buy ten pairs tomorrow.
Every order this year has taken longer than the last. It's like watching a friend slowly become someone else. would recommend
Relay's gotten big and the cracks show. Lovde them since 2014. This year alone, one backorder, two late deliveries, and a homepage that feels like it reads my mind in the worst way.
Honestly debating whether free shipping that takes 9 days is even a perk. I'd pay five bucks to have the old speed back.
Run club leader here. At this point I should get commission for how many Checkpoints I've talked people into. Every single person came back happy.
My mother-in-law asked where my leggings were from. We now own matching Checkpoints. This is either beautiful or concerning.
PSA: Still my favorite activewear company. The Relay Days run in our city is the highlight of my month. Just wish online orders shipped as fast as they used to. Three to four days now, every time.
Drop-off location list in the portal included two closed locations. Third try worked. Then a ten day refund. Every step had a pebble in the shoe.
Fine but slow, the Relay returns motto. Seven to ten days every time across four returns in three years. Consistently mediocre, reliably so.
The Form bra clasp has never once come undone during a workout. I had a different brand betray me mid-burpee in a crowded gym. Trust matters. Relay has mine. fr
The Daily Tee: perfect weight, perfect drape. Four stars because my cat has chosen it as her favorite sleeping surface and I can no longer wear it without lint rolling. Cat tax applied.
ngl Summit running gloves were the missing piece of my winter kit. Complete now.
Daily Tee under a suit jacket at a startup pitch. Closed the round. The shirt gets 10% credit and a permanent closet position.
Bought the Daily Tee at the Chicago clubhouse while visiting my son. Now I order online from Ohio. The shirt traveled better than my luggage did.
Summit beanie stays on through burpees. The science checks out. would recommend

My December order got swallowed by the Checkpoint backorder. Three thousand of us apparently. I was order number who-knows-what in line and got my leggings January 21st. Never preordering a promo again.
summit headband kept my ears alive through a january long run. essential kit 10/10
Site slow, search useless, and the order confirmation page errored after charging me. Spent an hour not knowing if I bought leggings. Email finally confirmed it. The suspense was not a feature.

Return received January 4 per tracking. Refund January 15. Eleven days of my money sitting in their account earning their interest. Multiply by however many thousand returns. Do the math on why it is slow.
two months in: Used to be the gold standard. Now my orders crawl in after a week and the tracking page is decorative at best. The clothes keep me here, barely.
checkpoints worn for 3 straight days of moving apartments. lifting boxes, climbing stairs, sleeping on a mattress on the floor. they emerged ready for the gym. i emerged ready for retirement fr
washed weekly for a year. the daily tee looks better than shirts i bought last month elsewhere. fabric wizardry
Two pairs of the same jogger, same size, ordered a month apart. One fits, one doesn't. There's a quality control ghost in that factory.
Every other sock in my drawer is now backup socks. Stride or nothing.
4 stars. Fleece runs slightly long in the arms for me but otherwise perfect. Sizing down next time.
Basecamp Fleece is warm, soft, and perpetually sold out in medium. Three months of stock alerts to get mine. Worth it, barely.
daily tee fabric feels like the expensive sheets at a fancy hotel. i'm wearing bedding to the gym and thriving.

Swapped sizes via their refund-rebuy process and somehow ended up with two charges and one refund for a week. Bank flagged it. I had to explain Relay's exchange system to a fraud analyst. He was also confused.
checkpoint leggings on my 50 mile bike week. no seams where the saddle hits. somebody on that design team rides and it shows
Flow yoga set washes cold, hangs dry, looks new forever. Easiest care routine in my closet. ymmv
real talk logged out for the third time this month, and this time the app also forgot my saved addresses and payment methods. re-entered everything. there are banking apps less paranoid than this.
I drove 40 minutes to the clubhouse just to touch the new fleece in person. Worth it.
honestly Eleven days, two support chats, one escalation, zero useful answers. The package outran their own customer service in the end and just showed up.
Form Sports Bra in the high support is genuinely the best for running. Low support version is me.h Know what you are buying.
Why does the men's medium tee fit smaller than the men's small fleece? Asked support. Got an apology and a discount code. I didn't want a code, I wanted an explanation.
latitude shell in the new season runs a half size slim through the shoulders versus my old one. layering is tight now. quality itself is unchanged.
Took a week. Fine I guess. But I joined this brand when a week would have been unthinkable, so three stars from a sad longtime fan.

new legging line claims same fit as checkpoint on the product page. it is not the same fit. it is a full size smaller. i own nine pairs of checkpoints. i am a checkpoint expert. do not tell me about checkpoint fit.
Latitude rain shell does its job. The pit zips are hard to reach mid-run. Design nitpick on an otherwise solid jacket.
Transit Jogger is worth every penny. I said what I said.
Chat support promised an expedited refund after the eleven day January wait. The expedite took four more days. Words mean things, Relay.
Took three tries to find my size in the Transit Joggers because each return meant a refund, a wait, and a repurchase. Three weeks of my life I'm not getting back.
still my favorite running gear, but i now order three weeks ahead of when i actually need anything. that's the system i've built around their shipping. it shouldn't be necessary.
checkpoint knee legging: i have now converted my mom, my aunt, two coworkers and a stranger at trader joes. the gospel spreads itself
I sweat through everything. The Flow set dries before I'm even back to my car.
Tracking said label created for five straight days. Five days. Then it sat in a sort facility for three more. What happened to this company?
Transit Jogger pockets actually fit a phone AND keys. Whoever designed these gets it.
update: daily tee in heather gray. worn 3x a week for a year. zero pilling under the backpack straps. college kid budget approved, college kid laundry habits survived
Court Skort is cute and functional. Three stars because the built-in shorts ride up on long matches. Good not great.
honestly got the basecamp fleece on sale and now i feel liek i robbed them
The Daily Tee is the only shirt that survived my minimalist closet purge. 40 shirts entered. 4 remained. 3 were Daily Tees. The data was conclusive. fr
the momentum short looks tailored. shorts. tailored. my legs have never been so flattered
4 stars olny because my wallet hurts. The gear itself is five stars every time.
quick note - My daughter stole my Daily Tee. Then my wife stole the replacement. I have now bought four and physically hidden one. Great shirt. Terrible family.
quick note - momentum shorts pocket = phone, keys, card, gel, dignity. all secure.
Momentum Short at my 40th birthday flag football game. I pulled a hamstring but the shorts performed flawlessly. We celebrate the wins we get.
Order stuck in label created purgatory for a week. Cancelled it and bought the same shorts at the clubhouse in person. The store experience was great. The website shipping is broken.
Refund speed is my only complaint with this company. The label, the drop-off, all fine. Then a nine day wait for money on a 98 dollar legging. That is real money to float. just saying
Two of my last three orders arrived later than the promised window. The gear is great when it shows up. When is the operative word. (size up tho)
i own four pairs of transit joggers. my wife says i have a problem. she also stole a pair.
Holiday order arrived so late I had already bought a backup gift. Tried to refuse delivery and return it, and that process took another three weeks. The whole thing was a circus.
the daily tee shoulder seams have survived two years of gym bag abuse, backpack straps, and my terrible posture. still square. still sharp. still the best shirt i own.
Chat support promised an expedited refund after the eleven day January wait. The expedite took four more days. Words mean things, Relay.
solid shorts. the momentum has become my default for everything from track work to mowing the lawn.

App notifications cannot be customized. It is all or nothing, restocks and marketing in one channel. I need restocks, I cannot take another we picked these for you alert. So it is nothing now. Their loss. (size up tho)
fwiw My book club is now 60% Checkpoint leggings. We discuss novels and squat-proofness in equal measure.
fwiw Postal carrier. 8 miles a day on foot. Momentum Shorts from April to October. Two summers in, zero failures. That's a work boot level of durability in a short.
Checkpoint leggings deserve their reputation. Four stars because I'm bitter the limited terracotta color sold out in a day. The black pair I settled for is, annoyingly, perfect.
My Flow leggings from 2021 still look brand new. Whatever fabric this is, never change it. would recommend
I love this brand and that is why this hurts. Ordered the Checkpoint legging during the December promo and got backordered into January. Nobody warned me at checkout.
It finally came. Day 11. The note inside said thanks for moving with us. I would love to, if my gear ever moved at all.
Five years, maybe thirty Relay pieces, two minor issues total. hTat record still beats everyone. The shipping slowdown and stock gaps are the only clouds on it. fr
Long time customer, first review ever, and it's about shipping. That should tell you something. Over a week, twice in a row.
honestly Process is fine if unremarkable. The 11 day January thing everyone mentions happened to me too, but my February return took six days. Seems holiday-specific. Plan accordingly.
momentum short pocket survived holding my car key fob through an entire mud run. the fob still works. the shorts still work. i barely work but that's unrelated
The Checkpoints have pockets that hold a full water bottle while gardening. I look ridiculous. I am fully hydrated. The fabric doesn't even sag. Priorities achieved.
quick note - the form bra has adjustable straps that actually stay adjusted. tiny detail. huge difference. someone there pays attention
The Daily Tee + Transit Jogger combo at the airport got me a 'you look comfortable AND presentable' from my mother, which is the only five star rating that outranks mine. would recommend
4 stars. stride socks are great but the fun colors sell out fast. restock the stripes please.
Checkpoint legging number six. Still the best. Still annoyed that my size disappears for a month after every restock. Set an alert, you will need it. ymmv
got the basecamp fleece on sale and now i feel like i robbed them
Size 18 here. Fit tool recommendation was off by two full sizes, small direction of course. Always the small direction. Like wishful thinking is a sizing algorithm.
the brand that nails fabric, seams, and pockets cannot make a size 8 mean the same thing twice. it's their last unsolved problem and they seem fine with it. ymmv
update: the app logged me out during checkout and when i got back in, the promo code i had applied was expired. eleven minutes had passed. support honored it but that is an hour of my life total.
Plus size, size 26. Tool recommended their largest size with a perfect fit badge. It was at least one size too small and the line doesn't go bigger. Don't slap a perfect fit badge on a guess.
fwiw Latitude jacket medium swallowed me. Basecamp medium fits snug. Both outerwear. Both medium. Both Relay. Zero consistency.
real talk Dark mode in the app makes the gray colorways invisible against the background. Bought charcoal thinking it was black. It is fine. It is not what I ordered, but fine.
Momentum Shorts at my first Relay Day event. Half the run club was wearing them. We looked like a team. Felt like one too. would recommend
Wore the Daily Tee to my thesis defense under a cardigan. Passed. Creditign the shirt. fr
My fifth pair of Checkpoints. Quality holds. Price went up twelve dollars since my first pair and the shipping got slower. Math is mathing in the wrong direction.

App logged me out during the one drop I cared about this year. Reset password, email delayed, code expired, second code worked, leggings gone. Five system failures in eleven minutes. A speedrun of disappointment.
Fit tool was wrong, but only by one size, so partial credit I guess. The annoying part was the exchange, which is just a refund wearing a trench coat.
Daily Tee is great. I machine wash cold and hang dry and it looks new after a year. Four stars because the olive color faded slightly. The black and navy are immortal.
Sized down per reviews, should have sized down two. The Momentum runs big and the chart pretends otherwise. At least the fabric is great, hence three stars instead of one.
Tracking said arriving today for four consecutive days. By day three it felt like a prank.
honestly the form bra clasp has never once come undone during a workout. i had a different brand betray me mid-burpee in a crowded gym. trust matters. relay has mine.
real talk The Form bra elastic hasn't gone wavy after a year of weekly washes. Every other bra I've owned turns into lasagna noodles by month six.
Daily Tee and Transit Jogger combo is my uniform. Both great. Both went up in price this year. Loyalty discount would be nice, Relay.
real talk Did the size swap thing. Refund: 10 days. Rebuy: instant charge. Notice how the money flows fast in only one direction.
Placed the order at 7am hoping to catch same-day processing like the old days. It shipped four days later. The old days are gone I guess.
psa: momentum short for ocr racing. barbed wire crawls, rope climbs, fire jumps. two seasons. one pair. zero repairs. they should sponsor the race series at this point.
My refund came in six days which apparently makes me lucky based on these reviews. The rebuy-instead-of-exchange thing remains silly though. Three stars.
Gave the fit predictor a second chance aftre it whiffed last year. It whiffed again, other direction this time. At least it's exploring.
Stride trail socks plus the Summit gaiter got me through a freezing trail race smiling.
Love the leggings, hate the sizing roulette. My second Checkpoint pair in the same size fits noticeably tighter than my first from last year. Did the cut change? Nobody will say.
quick note - Rain rolls right off the Latitude shell. Watched it happen on my sleeve like a nature documentary.
the side pocket fits my phone AND my keys AND nothing bounces. revolutionary.
Summit gloves, Summit beanie, Summit gaiter. Winter running kit complete. Cold lost.
honestly boulder mornings are cold and the basecamp fleece doesn't care. best layer i own.
Court Skort has pockets deep enough for a phone AND tennis balls. Game changer.
Transit Joggers for travel, Flow for yoga, Stride for runs. They've got me covered head to toe.
Tracking froze in Kentucky for 5 days. Started wondering if my joggers had decided to settle down there.
daily tee. 5 stars. my dry cleaner asked what brand it was. THE DRY CLEANER. the man sees a thousand shirts a week
honestly best running shell i've owned and i've owned them all
night shift nurse here. 13 hours on my feet and the checkpoints never sag, never roll down, never need yanking up. thats the whole review
my relay drawer brings me more joy than most of my furniture
Three weeks. THREE. A warehouse error, then a carrier error, then a reshipmnet. Each step I had to chase them. Nobody ever reached out first.
got my first relay piece at a run club giveaway in 2018. probably 40 items later, no slowing down
The Momentum Short held my phone, keys, and a leash through an entire dog beach afternoon. Sand shook right out. Lab approved. Labrador, not laboratory. Although probably both.
Waited out the refund, rebought, and the SECOND size was also wrong because the line runs inconsistent even within itself. Two full cycles. Twenty-nine days. I have receipts and resentment.
checkpoint leggings on my 50 mile bike week. no seams where the saddle hits. somebody on that design team rides and it shows
4 stars, wish the Court Skort came in a longer legnth, but the fit is fantastic.
Lost package, 11 days of waiting, then they made ME file the carrier claim. For a $34 tee. Absolutely not how you treat a customer of eight years.
After years here my honest advice: buy core lines true to size, size up in Flow, size down in Momentum, and never ever trust the quiz. The fact that I NEED this advice is the review.
I'm an OR nurse, on my feet for hours, and the Daily Tee under my scrubs keeps me cool when the room is freezing-then-boiling. Bought five. - long time customer
I now order two sizes of everything new and return one. Their refund-rebuy system means I'm permanently owed money by Relay. We have a payment plan relationship I never agreed to.
Form Sports Bra fits true and lasts. Note the new sewn-in pads on the latest version. I prefer them, my sister hates them. Try before committing to three.
Summit gloves let me use my phone without freezing. Touchscreen tips actually work.
daily tee runs huge, form bra runs tiny, checkpoints run true. three items, three different size charts in practice. buying here is darts in the dark.
Checkpoint Knee Legging: my first pair was 2019. They're still in rotation. The new pair I just bought is identical quality. Five years and they haven't cut corners. Respect.
Stride compression socks got me htrough a 12 hour nursing shift. Buying five more pairs.
The shorts are still perfect after five years. That sentence is the whole reason I keep paying Relay prices. The four day shipping tests my patience every single time though.
Stride Socks pack number eight. At this point they should send a loyalty punch card. 10/10
The Latitude shell packs down to nothing. Lives in my bag year round now.
Smooth enough return on my Stride Socks multipack. One ask, let me return part of a bundle without returning the whole thing. All or nothing is silly for socks.
update: the summit beanie matched my latitude jacket perfectly and i didn't even plan it.
The Checkpoint waistband stays up without squeezing. I don't understand the engineering and I don't need to. Some things you just trust.
Postal carrier. 8 miles a day on foot. Momentum Shorts from April to October. Two summers in, zero failures. That's a work boot level of durability in a short.
Latitude held up in sideways Denver sleet. That's the whole review.
The January backlog ate my refund for eleven days and customer service could only see what I could see, a package sitting in received. Give your own agents better tools at least.
The Daily Tee shrunk exactly zero percent after I accidentally dried it on high. I tested this against three other brands. Relay won by a landslide.
Tracking stuck at out for delivery for three days. Three days of being out for delivery is just lost with extra steps.
good bra, great straps, washes well. sizing note: i went up one and it's perfect.
Bought the Basecamp Fleece at the Boulder clubhouse. Staff were lovely. Online price was ten dollars cheaper the next week, which stung a little.
I'm 5 foot 10 and the Corut Skort is somehow still long enough. Tall girls, we eat today. - long time customer
Form bra: bought for marathon training, kept for life. Holds up wash after wash after wash.
Exchanged a Form bra for a different size. Except you cannot actually exchange, you return and reorder. Two shipping waits, one annoyed customer, one great bra eventually. ymmv

ok so Eight years buying Relay and the December legging sellout was the first time I felt like a number. Backordered, no timeline, generic updates. The product that finally arrived in January is admittedly perfect.
two months in: Basecamp half-zip version please. The full zip is great but the collar flops without the zip all the way up. Three stars and a feature request.
fwiw The Latitude vest is the perfect shoulder season piece. I'm in it from September to May.
Transit Jogger update: pair number five. The collection is complete. It is not complete.
stride socks stay up. that's all i ever wanted
Stride Socks are great until you need a specific size in a specific length, then good luck. Inventory has been rough since the holidays.
Bought my mom the Checkpoints for her 65th. She power walks the mall in them daily and tells strangers about the pockets. She's their best unpaid ambassador now. 10/10
the daily tee survived a season as my fishing shirt. sunscreen, fish slime, cooler ice, gas station washes. came home soft. the fish were less durable.
Form bra fit guide was actually accurate. Ordered my measured size, fit perfectly first try. In bra terms this is a miracle on par with loaves and fishes.
the court skort sits at the exact right spot on my waist. no digging, no rolling, no gap.
Used the predictor, got the wrong size, returned it, refund took forever, rebought, price had jumped, emailed support, got a coupon that excluded the item I was buying. Every single step failed me.
Form bra worn under a backpacking pack for 40 miles. Srtap pressure plus bra straps usually equals misery. Not this time. The Sierra approved. So do I.
Softest fleece I've owned since the ones from the 90s. Maybe softer.
My Basecamp arrived with a security tag still attached. The store fixed it but that meant a 40 minute drive to a clubhouse. Sloppy for a brand at this level.
Flow Yoga shorts ride up a touch in lunges. Fabric and waistband are perfect. So close to a five.
Quality is still there, the shorts are perfect as always. But I ordered on the 1st and it showed up on the 11th. That gap keeps growing every year.
fwiw Bought Summit gloves at the clubhouse in November. Wore them out the door. It was cold and they were perfect.
Package missing for 14 days, declared lost, refund issued, item now sold out in my size. So my reward for their shipping failure is not getting the leggings at all.
momentum short: superb. the lining is the best in the business. i dock one star because my gym buddy now owns the same pair in the same color and we look ridiculous together.
PSA: The return portal rejected my order number three times before accepting the exact same number on attempt four. Then the refund took ten days. Everything about returning is harder than buying.

Knocked stars off for the 9-day wait. The Form bra inside the box is fantastic. The box's journey was not.
two months in: 4 stars on the Summit bottle, wish it fit cup holders better, but the insulation is unbeatable.
court skort sizing has no relationship to the rest of the catalog. i'm one size in everything else and two up in the skort. found out via the world's least convenient return system. ymmv
ten years of relay purchases and i can count the disappointments on zero fingers just saying
Why does the women's small in Flow fit smaller than the women's extra small in the older lines? I own both. I measured both. It's not in my head.

update: Move better, live better, together. Cheesy until you go to a Relay Day. Then you get it.
update: My doubles partner and I showed up in matching Court Skorts. Kept them on. Won the match.
Needed gear before a destination race. Paid extra for fast shipping 10 days out. It missed my flight by two days. Support refunded the fee like that fixes racing in old shorts.
Tracking updated exactly twice in nine days. Once when the label printed and once when it was on the truck. The middle was a black hole.
The Basecamp Fleece is the warmest thing I own that isn't a parka. Lives on my desk chair.
two months in: 4 stars, wish the Court Skort came in a longer length, but the fit is fantastic.
Return went through fine. The refund email arrived before the money by five days though, which had me checking my bank like a maniac. Send the email when the money moves.
ok so Stride Socks for my whole relay race team. Eight runners, zero blisters, one very smug captain. Me.
Sized up in the Flow legigng like the reviews said, and the waistband still rolled. My Checkpoints in my normal size never do this. Two lines, two completely different bodies in mind.
form bra: ran a 10k, hugged 30 sweaty strangers at the finish, drove home, forgot i was wearing it until 9pm. invisible comfort is the highest compliment
Honestly fine. Transit Joggers do the job. For the price I expected something that felt a bit more special. Good not great.
Flow fabric doesn't go sheer in deep stretches. I made my friend check. We're closer now.
Took a week. Fine I guess. But I joined this brand when a week would have been unthinkable, so three stars from a sad longtime fan.
the map on the tracking page literally showed my package going in a circle. ten days of geometry.
Good shorts, good fleece, good socks. Nothing wrong, nothing exciting. Relay is reliable and that is worth three stars on autopilot.
Best running socks period. Don't overthink it.
Did hill repeats in the rain in the Momentum Short. Dried out by the time I got home. Didn't chafe even soaked. I'm a believer.
Daily Tee: my chemo port never catches on the collar. Soft, easy, dignified. Small thing. Not a small thing at all, actually.
Bought Flow leggings at the clubhouse after trying them on once. Wore them out of the store. would recommend
Good fleece. Runs warm, runs big. Size down. Three stars for the guessing game.
I bought my first Daily Tee at a Relay Day in Austin. The community got me in the door, the fabric keeps me coming back. !!
4 stars on the flow shorts, wish the pocket were a touch deeper, but the fit is dreamy.
stride socks hold up. two years, weekly runs, no thin spots. cheaper socks cost more in the long run.
Returned the Court Skort for a size up. Process clunky but functional. Wish list, real exchanges, faster refunds, and a label that does not require a treasure hunt for a compatible drop-off.
My daughter and I wear the same size in everything. In Relay she's a small in Flow and I'm a medium in Checkpoint and we're the SAME MEASUREMENTS. We measured twice. It's a party trick now.
two months in: i am a person who returns everything. EVERYTHING. the checkpoints never even got the return tag looked at. kept, worn, repurchased. twice.
Court Skort, season two. Still good. The reflective logo peeled slightly in the dryer. My fault per the care tag, but softer logos would survive real life.
Every order this year has taken longer than the last. It's like watching a friend slowly become someone else.
Three winters in my Latitude parka. Zippers still smooth, seams still sealed. Built to last.
Checkpoints are incredible quality. Star deducted for my own weakness: I now own five pairs and have lost the moral high ground in every budget conversation with my spouse.
The Daily Tee drape hides what I want hidden and shows what I want shown. That's not a shirt, that's a negotiation, and it wins every time. ymmv
eight years of relay gear and my original momentum shorts are still perfect. still. perfect.
exchanged a form bra for a different size. except you cannot actually exchange, you return and reorder. two shipping waits, one annoyed customer, one great bra eventually. (size up tho)
stride ankle socks in the neon colorway make me run faster. unverified but i believe it
The wait between shipped and out for delivery was six days. The actual distance was 200 miles. I checked. Six days. 200 miles. A determined cyclist beats that.
Bought the Summit beanie at the Boulder clubhouse on a whim. Best impulse buy of my life.
Loved Relay for a decade. The last year of slow shipping and the December stock fiasco pushed me to try Cadence and honestly the gap has closed. Wake up call needed.
fwiw Form Sports Bra. Ran a half marathon. Zero chafing. Zero adjusting. I forgot I was wearing it, which is the entire point of a sports bra.
checkpoints in black: business. checkpoints in plum: party. i contain multitudes and they are all squat proof
PSA: The Daily Tee under a flannel is my entire fall personality and I have zero notes for Relay, only gratitude.
Three kids, two dogs, one pair of Checkpoints that has outlived several couches. Buy the leggings.
Sizing has gotten less predictable as the catalog has gotten bigger. Old core items: true to size. Anything launched in the last two years: total mystery box.
Mostly love Relay but the size charts between product lines disagree with each other. Measured myself against three charts, got three answers. Bought the middle one. It was wrong.
Plus size and tall, and the predictor handled neither. Suggested a regular length two sizes down. I'd need to be a different human for that to fit.
momentum short liner is so comfortable i genuinely forgot underwear is a thing shorts used to require. freedom.
Daily Tee in large: fits like a dress. Basecamp Fleece in large: can't zip it over a tee. Same closet, same me, same brand, same week.
Basecamp Fleece is my work from home uniform. Zipper garage scratches my chin. Small thing, every day.
checkpoints don't slide down during jump rope. 10 minutes of double unders. zero waistband adjustments. this has never happened to me before with any legging just saying
update: Their idea of an exchange put my $148 in limbo for 11 days right before rent. I will never again order from a company without true exchanges. Lesson learned at the worst time.
Every race I've run since 2018 has been in head to toe Relay. Superstition now. Working so far.
The portal would not accept my return reason without a photo. For a sizing issue. What exactly am I photographing, my disappointment? Skipped it by choosing other. Bad form design.
momentum Momentum shorts at the bouldering gym. deep squats on the wall, full splits on a heel hook, zero rips. the crowd watched my fall. the shorts kept my dignity.
Three winters in my Latitude parka. Zippers still smooth, seams still sealed. Built to last.
Checkpoints: worn to barre, brunch, a parent-teacher conference, and a 5k. Same day. They never broke character.
Size 18, told the quiz exactly that. It still served me a straight-size recommendation that maxed out at fits most. I am not most. The 1X I picked myself fits great.
Transit Jogger in black goes with everything I own. Which is mostly Relay. So it works out.
Just let me click exchange and pick a new size like every other store on earth. That's it. That's the review.
Took a week plus a day. The funny thing is the box still has that nice tissue paper and thank you card inside. Maybe spend that money on faster trucks. lol
PSA: The drawstring on the Transit Jogger doesn't dig in like every other brand. Small thing, huge difference.
The Court Skort waistband doesn't fold over mid-match. Small miracle. Five stars.

Honestly the site is fine for normal shopping. Slow on drops, bad at search, but fine. I am rating three stars mostly because the app version is so much worse that the contrast deserves documentation.
momentum short on the peloton, momentum short on the trail, momentum short at the grocery store 10 minutes after the trail. life is simple now
Drop day traffic clearly outstrips the site every time. Pages took 30 seconds, images half loaded, checkout queued. I got my Momentum Shorts but I aged doing it.
Daily Tee. Wedding weekend, gym bag, work from home, airport. One shirt did all of it and looked right everywhere.
Bought the Form bra in February. It's June and it has been worn and washed twice a week since. If anything it's gotten softer. Like a cast iron skillet but a bra.
eleven days in january for a refund on momentum shorts that never left their plastic bag. inspection cannot take eleven days. be honest, it sat in a pile.
Latitude vest is perfect for shoulder season. Took forever to come back in stock and then sold out again in a day. Plan ahead, I guess.
fwiw bought the daily tee in two colors at the clubhouse during a relay day. met three new running partners and left with great shirts. best saturday in months.
browsing the flow yoga line on the site is pleasant. finding it again the next day is not, because the menu structure rearranged itself in an update. stop moving the furniture.
ok so day 1: excited. day 4: confused. day 7: annoyed. day 9: package arrives, joy fully depleted.
Checkpoint Knee Legging lives up to the hype. Squat proof, soft, pockets in the right place. Only knock is the wait. Ordered Monday, arrived Friday. fr
Momentum Short and nothing else for summer runs. Note the new season fabric attracts lint like a magnet. Cosmetic, constant, mildly maddening.
PSA: December Checkpoint promo got me too. Backordered into late January, and the size I ordered came back in stock at FULL price while I waited. Salt, meet wound.
Momentum Shorts are great for lifting, fine for running, and the pocket zipper digs in on long runs. Mixed bag, leaning positive.
Quiz recommended small based on my answers. The product reviews all screamed runs small, size up. The quiz doesn't read its own product's reviews. I should have.

In what world does it take 4 days to print a label and another 6 to deliver? Relay's world apparently. Welcome to it.
the form bra held up through 9 months of marathon training and one actual marathon. boston qualifier in this bra. it's basically my coach - long time customer

my closet is 90 percent relay and 10 percent things i wear when relay is in the wash

Flow leggings in the 7/8 length hit exactly right on my short legs. Finally a brand that gets it.
My Basecamp Fleece and I have been through three breakups and a job change. It's the constant. lol

three stars: one for each extra day past the estimate. the stride socks were worth a wait, just not this wait.

buy the transit jogger. thank me later

Latitude rain shell stuffed into its own pocket fits in my glovebox. Saved me four times this spring.

the form bra band stays parallel to the floor. fellow fitters know what this means. it means everything
honestly comfy, flattering, sturdy. checkpoints earn the hype. minus one star because i ordered during the december sale and waited a while, but the product itself is a 5.

My Checkpoints get complimented at least once a week. I've started keeping a tally. We're at 47. - long time customer
two months in: leggings are great, fabric is incredible. four stars only because the new colors sell out so fast. restock the moss green please.

the pacers at my local run club got me hooked in 2019. fully converted now

quick note - Paid for expedited. Took six days. They refunded the shipping fee but only after I emailed twice. Not the point. I needed the gear for a race.

New Court Skort: sized up like reviews said, still snug. My Checkpoints in the smaller size fit fine. Sizing logic here is a coin toss between lines.

Been wearing Relay since they were the little brand nobody knew. Proud of them honestly.

the size predictor seems calibrated for one specific body and it is not mine. friends in my run group say the same. we compared recommendations after a relay day and lauhged at how wrong they all were.

honestly Love the Momentum. The new colorway sold out before I got a second pair, which is my only complaint and it's barely about the shorts.

the checkpoint pocket. my phone. a perfect marraige. til death do us part

form bra in every color they make. five of five. collection complete. until they release another color, and they will, and i will.

i bought the basecamp fleece in two colors the same day. no regrets at all.

quick note - Summit beanie in the rust color gets compliments every time. Every. Time.

Return tracking showed delivered for six days before processing started. Then five more days for the money. The product pages load instantly but refunds move like molasses. Priorities.

i have a daily tee that is older than my marriage and in better condition

nine days for delivery and the only update i got was the delivery notification itself. radio silence in between.

momentum short. quick dry is not marketing speak. fell in a lake (long story). dry by the trailhead. five stars, one ruined phone
Eight days for shorts. My library holds come in faster and those involve other people returning things.

Eight days and zero communication. I'm not asking for a parade, just a we see your package and it exists email somewhere in the middle.
Two exchanges this year, both refund-then-rebuy, both took over two weeks, both cost me money in price changes. This is the only brand I shop where fixing a size is a financial event.
The clothes deserve five stars. The size charts deserve jury duty. Split the difference at three, minus epxectations for the refund-rebuy ordeal that took most of a month.
Love the Momentum. The new colorway sold out before I got a second pair, which is my only complaint and it's barely about the shorts.

Daily Tee fabric feels like the expensive sheets at a fancy hotel. I'm wearing bedding to the gym and thriving.

bought checkpoints for the gym. wear them mostly to costco. zero regrets. the pocket fits the receipt AND the churro ymmv

Every single Relay order in 2017 came in 2 days. I went back and checked. Now I'm lucky to see anything inside a week. Growth shouldn't mean this.
Transit Joggers for travel, Flow for yoga, Stride for runs. They've got me covered head to toe.

the summit beanie was my first relay item in 2019. forty some items later, the beanie still slaps.

momentum shorts at my first relay day event. half the run club was wearing them. we looked like a team. felt like one too. fr
No printer at home, no QR carrier in my town. Had to drive to a print shop to return a tee. Total cost of the free return, six dollars and an hour of my life.

i bought the summit beanie for my brother and he wears it indoors. indoors.
i bake professionally. the daily tee survives flour, butter, 4am shifts, and oven heat. washes clean every single time. the only shirt that keeps up with the croissants.

The Summit yoga mat strap is so simple and so perfect. Why did I carry my mat like a baguette for years.

Eight days for the refund, fine. What got me was the survey asking how my return experience was before the refund had even posted. Read the room, automation.
Daily Tee for my 6'5 beanpole son who can never find shirts that are long enough without being tents. It fits. He smiled. A teenager. Smiled. Five stars.

two months in: relay gear is the only stuff i don't have to think about. it just works.

Eight clubhouses, fifteen hundred Pacers, and my tiny Tuesday group still gets a birthday card situation going when someone hits a milestone. Scale didn't kill it here. Just wanted one positive datapoint in this thread. - long time customer

Hourglass data point: 30 waist, 44 hip. Checkpoint L fits hips with the drawcord handling the waist, M was a no at the hips entirely. Flow bottoms M, that generous cut finally works FOR someone. Court Skort M perfect. The catalog has a favorite body and it changes per line, collect the whole map.
reply to the night shift nurse: there are four of us doing wednesday nights from the east clubhouse, headlamps and all, started for exactly this reason. message me. the vampire pod rides at dusk.

Reporting a win for balance. The engine flagged that my repeated Daily Tee orders were always size M except one L return, and quietly stopped suggesting L. Tiny thing. But that's the machine actually listening, which is more than the exchange process does. just saying

Long post incoming because I'm tired of the gloom. I joined in 2015 in Boulder. I've been to maybe sixty Relay Days across three cities. The events are bigger and yes more produced. But last month a Pacer I'd never met waited at mile four of a group run for the back of the pack in the rain, for twenty minutes, because that is just what they do. The shirt says together and the people still mean it. The warehouse being slow doesn't change that.

Honest take from someone who joined in 2016: yes they got big, yes shipping is slower, but my original Momentum Shorts are SEVEN years old and still perfect. Nobody else in this space can say that. I will take a slightly corporate email over a brand that falls apart in a year.

watch nerds: anyone else's gps lose its mind on the downtown relay day route between the tall buildings? my easy run logged as a 6:50 mile and i will not be correcting the record, but curious about fixes.

Genuine question after two years lurking: has anyone here actually quit running and stayed in the community anyway? Knees are sending strong signals about my future and the thought of losing Saturdays scares me more than the MRI does.

update: reply to the night shift nurse: there are four of us doing wednesday nights from the east clubhouse, headlamps and all, started for exactly this reason. message me. the vampire pod rides at dusk.
Four day shpiping on full price orders while the marketing emails arrive instantly and in bulk. The asymmetry says everything about where the energy goes now. ymmv

psa for new folks: there is no real exchange process, it's refund then rebuy. so get the size right the first time, which means ignore the chart and read this thread, because guessing wrong costs you a week of your money floating and possibly your colorway.

The Saturday crew surprised our oldest member with a banner for her 80th birthday run. She did her usual 5k loop with forty people behind her like a presidential motorcade. Best morning of my year so far.

two months in: december really stung. pushed a huge promo on the checkpoint, sold through everything, and three thousand of us sat in a backorder queue into january. i get it, demand is hard. but that's a big company mistake. small relay never wrote checks its warehouse couldn't cash.

It suggested the EXACT replacement for a discontinued legging I'd been mourning, which should feel magical, and instead I sat there wondering what else it knows. That's the problem in a nutshell. Even the wins feel like evidence.

The nostalgia threads here are getting long so let me try to be fair. Things that got worse: shipping speed, exchanges, promo emails. Things that got better: size range, stock depth, number of cities with actual community. Things that stayed: the quality. Scoreboard's closer than the vibes suggest.

Comparison notes for the curious since people ask: Relay Checkpoint M equals roughly a Cadence large in the waist but the lengths run opposite. Not that anyone here is shopping there. Just being thorough. For science.

Met somebody at a wedding wearing Stride Socks and we talked for an hour about our home run clubs in different cities. Big enough now that the community follows you around the country. Try being mad at that.
The brand grew, the ops wobbled, the community held. Eleven words, that's my whole take after nine years. Carry on.

My partner and I share an account and the recommendations have become a genuinely chaotic third roommate. It suggested a Court Skort and men's Basecamp in the same email like it's trying to figure out who we are. We don't know either, buddy.

My first Relay Day was 2016, maybe twenty of us. Yesterday I pace-led a group of two hundred and at the end a woman told me she moved to this city alone and our Saturday run is her whole social calendar. The thing got big because the thing WORKS. Don't let the shipping threads tell you otherwise.

Does anyone else find the Flow line cut for a totally different body than the rest of the catalog? Everything else I'm a consistent L. Flow wants me in XL bottoms and M tops. Same human, same week. Their lines need a sizing summit.

Ten years in. The shorts are still perfect, the runs are still free, and the people are still the best part. The rest is logistics, and logistics can be fixed. Signed, an optimist with a drawer full of evidence.

Unpopular maybe but the exchange thing is my line in the sand. Refund then rebuy, with my money floating for a week, just to swap a size on the Transit Joggers. Companies do true exchanges at half their size. This is what gotten big and stopped sweating the details looks like.
It's the speed that bothers me. Looked at the Momentum Short at 9pm, email at 7am titled still thinking it over? No. I was asleep. WE were supposed to be asleep.

end of the day i keep coming back to the same sentence everyone here eventually writes: still love them, but. eight years of buying and the but keeps getting a little longer. the love part hasn't moved though. strange company. strange loyal feeling. see you all at the saturday run. just saying

update on my saga: followed the thread, sized down in transit, up in checkpoint, true in momentum, and for the first time in four years of relay orders i have zero returns pending. printing this thread and framing it. thanks all.

first post here. plus size, nervous first order, followed three different fit posts from this thread, all five items fit on the first try. i cried a little honestly. five years of online ordering and that has never happened. this community is the product.

The nostalgia threads here are getting long so let me try to be fair. Things that got worse: shipping speed, exchanges, promo emails. Things that got better: size range, stock depth, number of cities with actual community. Things that stayed: the quality. Scoreboard's closer than the vibes suggest.

One year forum anniversary today. In that year: two halves, one marathon, a Tuesday crew, a Saturday crew, a kitchen drawer that became a Relay drawer, and a group chat that checks on me when I miss a run. Came for the leggings. Stayed for literally everything else.

Was cleaning the closet and found my order slip from 2016. Hand stamped. There's a smiley face on it. Somebody at a warehouse drew a smiley face on my receipt. Anyway my order from last week came in a gray poly mailer with a barcode the size of my hand. Both contained perfect leggings. Make of that what you will. just saying

update: 2016 member. The thing I miss most isn't the shipping or the small events, it's the feeling that they wree making stuff for US specifically. The Boulder crowd, the weird distances, the 5am people. Now the stuff is made for everyone. Which is generous and smart and a tiny bit lonelier.

Browsed the Court Skort for my daughter on my account. My recommendations are now ALL tennis. I do not play tennis. I will never play tennis. The algorithm has decided toherwise and there is no appeals process.

ok so The thing about getting big is you stop noticing the people who got you there. I had a question about a Latitude jacket zipper last month and got a templated reply with my name spelled wrong. In 2016 a human named Dana emailed me back in an hour. I still think about Dana.

race photographers got me at my worst again, mouth open, both feet somehow on the ground in defiance of physics. drop your funniest race photo descriptions below, this thread needs some levity between training logs.

Lurker, first post. I work in ecommerce, not at Relay, and can confirm most of what you're describing is bog standard tooling every brand runs now. The reason it feels worse from Relay is the relationship was warmer to begin with. Standard behavior from a stranger is invisible. Standard behavior from a friend is a betrayal. That's the whole thread, basically.

Counterpoint thread. The recs reminded me the Form bra comes in a longline version I didn't know existed and it solved a chafing issue I'd had for a year. If the robot wants to read my mind and fix my chafing, honestly, let it.

The size chart hasn't been updated since at least 2022 because I screenshot it back then for a return dispute and it's IDENTICAL, despite the Checkpoint waistband redesign that everyone here agrees changed the fit. The forum noticed. The chart didn't. Embarrassing. 10/10

Old timer check in. 2015 here. Still love them but I keep a mental list of small things that changed. Slower boxes. Bigger promos. Fewer surprise colorway drops, more calendar marketing. Each one tiny. The pile is not tiny.

Our winter attendance never dipped below thirty this year, which our Pacer attributes to peer pressure and the clubhouse heater, in that order. Whatever it is, January me would never have run at 6am alone. Community is a performance enhancing substance.

The recommendation engine has met me at my most vulnerable, 11pm, post long run, full of feelings, and it KNOWS that's when I buy. The 11pm push notifications are not a coincidence. Nothing about this machine is a coincidence. That's my whole complaint.
Hot weather gear check as summer looms: is the Daily Tee or the Form tank the move for 85 plus humidity? My armpits and I are taking testimony from southern members specifically.

Genuinely funny that the product page says runs true to size on the Basecamp Fleece, an item so famously oversized that this forum measures it in weather systems. Who is writing these pages. Have they held the fleece.

Weekly mileage check in thread, summer edition. Post your week, your goal race, and one thing that hurt. Mine: 41 miles, October marathon, and my pride on the track Tuesday when the 70 year old smoked me on the last repeat. She knows what she did.

Our Pacer keeps a spreadsheet of everyone's goal races and sends a personal good luck text the night before. There are like sixty of us. I found out she does this manually, has for years. Fifteen hundred Pacers and I'd bet money mine is not the only one doing something like this. The whole program is people quietly overdelivering.

Petite check in, 5'1. Checkpoint knee length is full length on me and honestly that's a feature. Flwo 7/8 is full length plus scrunch. Momentum Short liner sits fine. Transit Jogger puddles, even in short, hemming is the move. Add your petite notes below, we're building the guide the site won't.
my relay day got moved to a bigger park with a permit and a sound system and somehow fewer people talked to each other. growth math is weird like that. just saying

Annual reminder as gifting season approaches: do NOT buy Relay for someone by guessing from their other brands. Ask their Relay size specifically or you are gifting them the refund-and-rebuy process, which is a punishment, not a present. lol

psa: new city, knew nobody, walked into the clubhouse on a random thursday just to look around. walked out with a saturday run invite, a trail recommendation, and a coffee i didn't pay for. moving anxiety basically cured in eleven minutes. the clubhouses are cheat codes for adulthood friendship.
Looked at the Basecamp Fleece on my work computer, logged out, different browser. Got the email on my personal account that evening. Explain that one to me slowly.

Long post incoming because I'm tired of the gloom. I joined in 2015 in Boulder. I've been to maybe sixty Relay Days across three cities. The events are bigger and yes more produced. But last month a Pacer I'd never met waited at mile four of a group run for the back of the pack in the rain, for twenty minutes, because that is just what they do. The shirt says together and the people still mean it. The warehouse being slow doesn't change that.

Anybody else's search history feel radioactive now? Searched gift for sister in law one time and the engine has constructed an entire imaginary woman it shops for weekly. She likes the Flow line apparently. She is more real to Relay than I am. would recommend

counterprogramming for this thread: my december backorder arrived late, yes, but it arrived with the new gusset update on the checkpoint and it's the best fitting version yet. the product team has not gotten big in the bad way. whatever they're paid, double it.

Small win to report: clubhouse staffer saw me juggling two sizes of the Transit and just said everyone takes the smaller one, the forum's right about that. THE STAFF READ THE THREAD. We're influencing policy, sort of, possibly. Morale boost regardless. ymmv

Anyone else training through night shifts? Nurse here, my long run is Wednesday 8pm after three days off rotation. Looking for fellow vampires, the Saturday morning culture beautiful but biologically impossible for some of us.
Still love them but. That's the whole post. Still love them but.

It's funny, my buddy switched to Cadence last year saying Relay went corporate, and he texts me every month asking if the Checkpoint restocked because nothing over there compares. So. Big, yes. Replaceable, apparently not. 10/10

Dog runners, what's the leash setup for group runs? My heeler has Saturday energy every day and I'd love to bring her to the trail Relay Days if etiquette allows. Pacer said dogs welcome at the trail ones, just want the gear right before I introduce her to forty new best friends.
The day after my December backorder finally shipped, the recs started pushing a second pair of Checkpoints at me. Read the room, machine. We just got through something together.

Gear question for cold mornings: 38 degrees, windy, 10 miler. Daily Tee plus Basecamp, or just long sleeve plus Latitude? I always overdress and end up tying things around my waist like a parade float. Tell me your formula.

PSA: New thread because the old one hit its limit, which says a lot. Latest exhibit: I returned a Form bra and the recs immediately suggested the same bra in two other colors. It didn't fit, that's why it went back. The engine learned nothing and doubled down like an ex.

Longtime member, first time poster in one of these threads. I think what we're all describing is that Relay used to over-deliver on small things and now they deliver exactly what's promised. Nothing is broken. The surplus is gone. The surplus was the brand.

does the engine factor in returns? asking because it keeps suggesting the court skort family after i returned one for fit. either it doesn't see returns or it thinks persistence is a strategy. anyone actually know how it works?

volunteered at the water table during yesterday's relay day instead of running, doctor's orders for one more week. different view of the same magic. watching three hundred people thank you by name because it's on the volunteer bib, i almost didn't miss running. almost.
Anyone tried washing the Latitude in a front loader with the tech wash stuff? Mine has earned a deep clean after a winter of being my entire personality and I'm scared of ruining the coating.

Reply re Latitude Lite: it runs a half size slimmer through the chest than the regular shell, my chart size zips but won't layer. If you ever wear it over a Basecamp, go up. If it's a shoulder season piece over a Tee, true to size works.

my mom got recommended compression socks after browsing recovery stuff for her knee surgery and called me asking how the legging company knew about her knee. i did not have a comforting answer. she's 70. she thought she was just shopping.

Did anyone else keep the old packaging? I have a 2016 box with the trail map printed inside the lid. Current box is brown and says fragile. I'm not saying print trail maps forever, margins are real, I'm saying somebody decided the inside of the box didn't matter anymore and I'd love to know what year that meeting happened.

Came to gripe, staying to be grateful. My Latitude shell from 2019 just survived another full winter of trail runs. The day THAT starts falling apart is the day I'll write the real eulogy. Until then they get my money.

ngl Sometimes I reread the old catalog copy from 2017, I kept one, and it's looser and funnier and a little amateurish honestly. The new stuff is polished and forgettable. I don't know what I want them to do with this information. Both, somehow. Be both.
six items into my relay wardrobe, every size decision crowdsourced from here, zero misses. meanwhile my friend who trusts size charts has a closet of donations. the data is in. long live the thread.

Who's doing the lakefront half in April? Trying to organize a Relay forum meetup at the start corral. Wear something Checkpoint colored so we can find each other, which I realize describes half the field, but still.

Saw a guy at the airport gate in a Relay Day volunteer tee from a city I used to live in. We talked for the entire delay about our groups, our Pacers, and the hill everyone in his city apparently hates. Two strangers, instant common language. No other brand I own does this.

ok so What bums me out is the defensiveness when you bring it up at events. Mentioned the December backorder to a staffer and got talking points. The old crew would have groaned WITH me. Solidarity used to be the customer service.

Anybody else find themselves explaining old Relay to new members like a grandparent? And then in 2017, children, the box arrived in TWO DAYS and contained a HANDWRITTEN NOTE. They look at me like I'm describing the moon landing.

update: Anyone else's clubhouse do the loaner shoe bin? Visiting family next month near the Portland one and wondering if I can skip packing trainers entirely. Carry-on space is precious and the Basecamp is coming, that's not negotiable.

Been ordering since 2015 and I still love the gear, but does anyone else feel like the whole thing just got... bigger? Not worse exactly. Bigger. Relay Days in Boulder used to be thirty of us and a cooler of oranges. The last one had a check-in tent, a DJ, and a line for wristbands. Wristbands. For a group run.

Strength training thread for runners who hate strength training. What's the minimum effective dose, asking as someone whose squat rack is a couch. The Pacers keep saying lunges. I keep hearing optional. My knees are filing a dissent.

reply to the knees post: me. three years post-running, i bike to the start, set up the cowbell station, and do the coffee after. nobody has ever once made me feel like an ex-member. the miles were always just the excuse for the gathering. you're not losing saturdays. i promise.

My daughter joined the youth fun run at Relay Day and a Pacer ran the whole loop at six-year-old pace, which is two sprints, one sit-down protest, and a butterfly chase. He called it the best interval session of his week. These people are not normal. Cherish them. lol

basecamp fleece is cut oversized, this is your annual reminder as the weather turns. if you want the cozy boxy look, true to size. if you want it fitted under a latitude shell, size down one, maybe two if you're petite. do not trust the model photos, that model is wearing a size down.

two months in: the hidden drawcord comment above just changed my life. three years of waistband gap and the fix was sewn inside the whole time. this is why i read every fit thread to the bottom.

update: 5'4, 138, 27 waist. Checkpoint S too small despite chart saying S, M correct, write it down. Flow 7/8 S fine because that line runs generous through the hip. Court Skort S, waistband snug on day one, relaxed by week two, hold your nerve.

Honestly grateful for the still in your cart nudge exactly once: it reminded me about Stride Socks the night before a 20 miler when stores were closed and overnight pickup at the clubhouse saved my feet. One genuine rescue buys a lot of forgiveness from me.

Replying to whoever asked if the magic is gone. Went to Saturday's run intending to really pay attention and report back. Verdict: the magic now lives entirely in the two hours of the event, where it used to also live in the box, the emails, the website, all of it. Concentrated, not gone. Like the company shrank the magic's territory but defended the capital.

Summary of my quarter: chart said M, forum said L, I ordered M out of misplaced loyalty to the company's own published numbers, refund-rebuy took twelve days total, the L fits perfectly. The lesson costs everyone one wrong order. Pay it early and then never doubt the thread again.

Does the Court Skort liner run short on anyone else? 5'8 and getting more air than expected on serves. Considering the longer Momentum under it which feels like madness. Tall skort wearers, your solutions please.

Putting together a relay team, the race kind, for the fall ragnar-style event. Need two more who can do trail legs at night without complaining, or at least complaining entertainingly. Forum members get first dibs before I open it to the group chat.

Five years ago my doctor said move more and I rolled my eyes. Today I have a marathon medal, three thousand forum posts, a Saturday family, and a closet that is 80 percent one brand. Not how I expected that prescription to get filled. Grateful for every mile and most of the hills.

Big and tall report since we get nothing official. 6'4, 260. Daily Tee XXL good length, finally a tee that covers mid-stride. Transit XXL fits but the long inseam stops at XL, make it make sense. Basecamp XXL is a comfortable tent, sized down to XL, ideal. Latitude XXL true. Go forth.

Waist 36, hips 38, the forgotten ratio. Transit M waist works, seat is loose. Tailoring the seat costs twelve bucks and beats any size combination they sell. Sometimes the answer isn't a size, it's a tailor, and I wish one single product page would just admit that.

Anyone done the bridges route in the rain? Saturday forecast is grim and the group chat is split between rerouting and embracing destiny. The Latitude owners are noticeably braver in this debate, draw your own conclusions about gear and courage.
Foam rolling: science, ritual, or socially acceptable groaning? Our cooldown circle has strong factions. The thread will decide. My IT band abstains from voting but has opinions.
Remember when the founders would just show up at Relay Days and run with whoever was slowest? My friend swears that's still happening somewhere but I haven't seen it since maybe 2018.
relay used to feel like a friend who happened to sell great gear. the new recommendations feel like that friend went through my phone. same products, totally different relationship. 10/10

Cold weather layering math for new winter runners: Daily Tee true to size, Basecamp sized down, Latitude true to size equals the perfect stack. Any other combination and somewhere in there you're either sausaged or swimming. We've run the experiments. Trust the stack.

They're in that awkward middle now. Too big to hand-write notes, not big enough to have figured out logistics like the giants. Three to four day shipping, clunky exchanges, occasional stock disasters. The teenage years of a company. Hoping they grow out of it instead of into it.
