ok so First Relay Day yesterday. Showed up alone, terrified, slowest person there by a lot. Three people stayed at my pace the ENTIRE run and one of them invited me to the beginners group like it was nothing. I get it now. I finally get it.

Milestone post: 1000 miles logged since my first Relay Day two years ago. Same loop where I once walked the hills, ran every step of it this morning with the same three people who walked them with me back then. Keeping this short because we're getting pancakes. Together, obviously.

Reading this thread as someone who joined last year. For what it's worth, from the outside Relay still feels totally different from every other brand I buy from. The fact that you all are mourning a golden age while STILL showing up to the runs kind of proves the point. !!

quick note - i screenshot every uncanny rec and the folder is getting long. latest: suggested trail gaiters two days after i signed up for a trail race through a totally different website. my theories are getting less fun by the week.

just want to appreciate that i asked a fit question here at 9pm and had four detailed answers with measurements by morning, including one person who tried on two sizes at the clubhouse on their lunch break specifically to report back. where else does that happen. nowhere is where.

compression preference poll, because chart debates hide a real split: do you size checkpoint for race-day squeeze or easy-run comfort? i keep one m and one l and they're honestly different products. the single greatest sizing tip i ever got from this thread, own both moods.
Reply re yoga: our clubhouse class is half runners who can't touch their toes, you'll be among your people. The instructor calls us the rebar group with great affection. Come Thursday.

real talk daily tee fabric update for the long-timers: the 2025 batches are cut maybe a half inch slimmer in the body than the 2019 ones, compared mine flat-stacked. not shrinkage, mine are air dried. if your decade-old m is your reference point, the new m is a different shirt.
the day after my december backorder finally shipped, the recs started pushing a second pair of checkpoints at me. read the room, machine. we just got through something together.

Men's Form compression top, anybody? The chart says go by chest but the shoulders are the actual constraint. 42 chest, broad shoulders, the L chokes, XL fits like the L should. One data point, add yours.
Waited since December 19 for my backordered Checkpoints. They arrived January 27. They are, infuriatingly, perfect. This company is lucky it makes the best legging on earth.

The others are looking at this item right now banner needs to go. This is a sports bra page, not concert tickets. Manufactured panic from the company whose whole thing was move better live better TOGETHER. Together apparently now means competing for inventory.

real talk Replying to whoever asked if the magic is gone. Went to Saturday's run intending to really pay attention and report back. Verdict: the magic now lives entirely in the two hours of the event, where it used to alsooo live in the box, the emails, the website, all of it. Concentrated, not gone. Like the company shrank the magic's territory but defended the capital.

What's everyone's rotation looking like for marathon season? I'm doing long runs in the Checkpoint, speed work in Momentum, easy days in the Transit because comfort is a training variable too. Curious if anyone races in the Flow stuff or if that's strictly studio.

update: One year forum anniversary today. In that year: two halves, one marathon, a Tuesday crew, a Saturday crew, a kitchen drawer that became a Relay drawer, and a group chat that checks on me when I miss a run. Came for the leggings. Stayed for literally everything else.

My first Relay Day was 2016, maybe twenty of us. Yesterday I pace-led a group of two hundred and at the end a woman told me she moved to this city alone and our Saturday run is her whole social calendar. The thing got big because the thing WORKS. Don't let the shipping threads tell you otherwise.

Three or four days for shipping is fine for a normal company. Relay spent a decade telling us they weren't a normal company. That's the actual problem, the gap between the story and the tracking number.
anyone else notice the emails changed? they used to read like a buddy texting you about a new colorway. now there's a shop now button every two inches. not mad, just noticing.
My Relay Day got moved to a bigger park with a permit and a sound system and somehow fewer people talked to each other. Growth math is weird like that. (size up tho)
Devil's advocate from a busy parent: I have maybe nine minutes a week to shop. The recs being creepy-accurate means those nine minutes work. I'm not proud of the trade but I'd make it again.

Eight clubhouses, fifteen hundred Pacers, and my tiny Tuesday group still gets a birthday card situation going when someone hits a milestone. Scale didn't kill it here. Just wanted one positive datapoint in this thread. fr

Question for long-distance hikers crossing over: does the Momentum liner chafe past three hours? Trail runners here swear by it but backpacking pace is different friction. Considering sizing up for airflow versus a liner-less alternative. Anyone done twenty miles slow in them?

Funny experiment for anyone bored: I clicked one random product, a kids rain shell I think, every day for a week to confuse the engine. It now sends me the most beautifully unhinged emails. Chaos has a price and the price is my inbox.

New thread because the old one hit its limit, which says a lot. Latest exhibit: I returned a Form bra and the recs immediately suggestde the same bra in two other colors. It didn't fit, that's why it went back. The engine learned nothing and doubled down like an ex.

i don't think they sold out. i think they scaled. those are different things and this forum keeps mixing them up. slower shipping is a logistics problem, not a values problem. the values problem would be if the pacers disappeared, and they haven't.

came to gripe, staying to be grateful. my latitude shell from 2019 just survived another full winter of trail runs. the day that starts falling apart is the day i'll write the real eulogy. until then they get my money. - long time customer
Why does the site show me the thing I JUST bought for two weeks after I buy it. I have the Transit Joggers. They are on my obdy. Stop showing me the Transit Joggers. lol

Bought the Checkpoint in my forum-verified size during the December promo, backordered into January, and when it finally came the NEW waistband fit differently than my three old pairs. Same size on the tag. Different legging in practice. Now the whole thread is recalibrating. Chaos. would recommend

Who's doing the lakefront half in April? Trying to organize a Relay forum meetup at the start corral. Wear something Checkpoint colored sooo we can find each other, which I realize describes half the field, but still.
Relay used to feel like a friend who happened to sell great gear. The new recommendations feel like that friend went through my phone. Same products, totally different relationship.

quick note - Serious question, does anyone know how to turn the recommendation emails down without losing the restock alerts? I want exactly one robot watching the Checkpoint inventory for me and zero robots reviewing my browsing history.

December promo, sold out in hours, backorder email, refund offered like that fixes anything. I didn't want my money back, I wanted the leggings I planned to give my sister for Christmas. Big companies forget that orders are sometimes promises to other people.
honestly Eight years in and the Checkpoint Knee Legging is still the best thing I own. Growth complaints are valid but let's not pretend the product slipped. It didn't.
The flag for the June half meetup has been REVEALED in the group chat and it exceeds every expectation, that's all I'll say. Sixty-eight registered now. The start corral will know we have arrived.

psa: looking for a wednesday evening crew on the north side, the official relay day is saturdays here and my work schedule eats weekends. anyone running informal weeknight groups? will bring the post-run snacks, i'm known for the snacks.
My physical therapist asked what changed because my consistency went from twice a month to four days a week. The answer is I found people who notice when I don't show up. Prescribe THAT.
Noticed the recs get pushier near payday. Could be my imagination. Anybody who gets paid on the 15th want to compare email timestamps with me, for science? would recommend

December really stung. Pushed a huge promo on the Checkpoint, sold through everything, and three thousand of us sat in a backorder queue into January. I get it, demand is hard. But that's a big company mistake. Small Relay never wrote checks its warehouse couldn't cash.

real talk Did anyone else keep the old packaging? I have a 2016 box with the trail map printed inside the lid. Current box is brown and says fragile. I'm not saying print trail maps forever, margins are real, I'm saying somebody decided the inside of the box didn't matter anymore and I'd love to know what year that meeting happened.
Sunscreen that survives a sweaty 16 miler, go. The forum has solved harder problems. I believe in us.

as a plus size shopper the recs have actually been great for me, full stop. they surface which styles run generous way better than the size charts do. the algorithm has paid more attention to my fit needs than the website copy ever has.

Reply to tall skort: the M skort liner is a full inch longer than the S liner, way more than the chart spacing suggests. I sized up for exactly this reason and just cinch the waistband. Air problem solved, dignity restored.

the nostalgia threads here are getting long so let me try to be fair. things that got worse: shipping speed, exchanges, promo emails. things that got better: size range, stock depth, number of cities with actual community. things that stayed: the quality. scoreboard's closer than the vibes suggest.

one year forum anniversary today. in that year: two halves, one marathon, a tuesday crew, a saturday crew, a kitchen drawer that became a relay drawer, and a group chat that checks on me when i miss a run. came for the leggings. stayed for literally everything else.
my pacer drove forty minutes to bring loaner shoes to a first-timer whose luggage got lost before our saturday long run. in 2025. the soul is intact, people. the shipping department is just tired.
reply re yoga: our clubhouse class is half runners who can't touch their toes, you'll be among your people. the instructor calls us the rebar group with great affection. come thursday. (size up tho)

Big and tall report since we get nothing official. 6'4, 260. Daily Tee XXL good length, finally a tee that covers mid-stride. Transit XXL fits but the long inseam stops at XL, make it make sense. Basecamp XXL is a comfortable tent, sized down to XL, ideal. Latitude XXL true. Go forth.
The pushy recs plus the slow shipping is the worst combo. They can predict what I want before I want it but can't get it to my door in under four days. Clairvoyant in the front, dial-up in the back.

Six months ago I couldn't run a mile. Yesterday I did eight with the Sunday group and nobody made it weird that I used to be the walk-break person. Beginners reading this: the back of the pack is where the best conversations are anyway.

athletic thighs check in, the eternal struggle. checkpoint sized for my quads means waist gap, sized for waist means thigh sausage. the m with the inner drawcord cinched is the closest to peace i've found. transit jogger weirdly fits us better, the taper starts lower than it looks.

five years ago my doctor said move more and i rolled my eyes. today i have a marathon medal, three thousand forum posts, a saturday family, and a closet that is 80 percent one brand. not how i expected that prescription to get filled. grateful for every mile and most of the hills.

Anyone else getting the you might be running low emails? Got one about my Daily Tees almost exactly when my oldest one developed a hole. Half impressed, half unsettled. Mostly posting to see if the timing was a coincidence or if they're actually estimating wear.

My partner asked why I keep buying from Relay if I complain about them so much and I didn't have a great answer except the shorts are still perfect and the Tuesday run group is half my social life. So I guess they've got me.

long post incoming because i'm tired of the gloom. i joined in 2015 in boulder. i've been to maybe sixty relay days across three cities. the events are bigger and yes more produced. but last month a pacer i'd never met waited at mile four of a group run for the back of the pack in the rain, for twenty minutes, because that is just what they do. the shirt says together and the people still mean it. the warehouse being slow doesn't change that.

The 2016 era wasn't actually perfect, friends. Sizes ran out consatntly, the site crashed on drop days, and there was one clubhouse in one state. We remember the warmth and forget the friction. Now we have the inverse problem. Pick your era, every one has a tax. fr

Shoutout to the regular who maintains the unofficial fit spreadsheet, you know who you are. Sent it to my sister, first order, three items, ZERO returns. The site could never. The spreadsheet stays winning.
50 states marathon people, how do you afford it and also how do you DO it? Met one of you at Saturday's run, state number 31, completely normal looking human. Inspired and alarmed. Mostly inspired.

My order history with them goes back nine years and the new site greets me like a stranger with a coupon. The data is RIGHT THERE. Old Relay knew me with way less information.

Bracketing strategy for new folks, refined over years of this forum: order your forum-adjusted size plus one neighbor, NOT your chart size plus neighbor. Refund the loser immediately, the nine day float clock starts at the warehouse scan, not delivery. Optimzie the misery.

honestly Race report: spring 10k, 51:32, four minute PR. The Momentum Shorts did their thing, the Stride Socks did their thing, my stomach did NOT do its thing but we move. Next stop the June half. Thanks to this board for the taper advice.

The since you're training for a marathon email was the line for me. I AM training for a marathon. I never told them that. I bought gels from somewhere else and long socks from them and apparently that's enough. Impressive and absolutely none of their business.
The day after my December backorder finally shipped, the recs started pushing a second pair of Checkpoints at me. Read the room, machine. We just got through something together. - long time customer

Training log thread, week 7 of 18. Hit 32 miles, legs feel decent, the long run fueling is still a mystery I solve with gummy bears. Drop your week below, accountability works. !!

Replying to the ecommerce lurker above: that's exactly it. I don't get mad at the airline for this stuff. I get mad at Relay because Relay spent a decade telling me we were different. You can't run a friendship and a funnel on the same email address.

tried to do a simple size swap on transit joggers and the system made me get refunded and reorder, and the reorder shipped in four days, and the color sold out in between. that whole chain of events is the gotten big experience in miniature. lol

Tall runners thread. 6'1 here, 34 inseam. Transit Jogger in Long is actually long, bless them. Checkpoint Knee knee length hits me mid-shin which honestly works. Momentum Short 7 inch reads more like 5 inch on tall frames, plan accordingly. would recommend

The Sunday group waited out a thunderstorm in the clubhouse doorway this morning, all of us crammed in like commuters, and somebody started passing around banana bread she'd baked FOR THE GROUP. We never even ran. Ten out of ten Relay Day, zero miles.

honestly The gotten big feeling, summarized by my last three interactions: a marketing email at 6am, a four day ship time, and a returns portal that made me refund and rebuy to change a size. Zero humans involved in any of it. That's the change. The humans got optional.

inseam catalog update, measured with an actual tape: momentum short 5 inch and 7 inch as labeled. transit jogger regular 29, long 31.5. checkpoint knee, 17 on a size m, scales about half inch per size. flow 7/8, 25. adding to the master thread. tape doesn't lie. ymmv

quick note - Anyone else training through night shifts? Nurse here, my long run is Wednesday 8pm after three days off rotation. Looking for fellow vampires, the Saturday morning culture beautiful but biologically impossible for some of us.
Four day shipping on full price orders while the marketing emails arrive instantly and in bulk. The asymmetry says everything about where the energy goes now.

I miss when the order confirmation email was funny. Petty complaint? Absolutely. But the jokes left around the same time the shipping slowed down and I don't think that's a coincidence. That's what scale costs you, the person who wrote the jokes.

joined 2019 so i caught the tail end of the small era. my take: the gear got better, the experience got flatter. the flow yoga line didn't even exist abck then and it's excellent. but ordering used to feel like a tiny event and now it feels like ordering. ymmv

Reply re youth XL vs adult XS: nearly identical chest, adult XS is two inches longer and the fabric is the heavier adult weight. For a growing kid the XS lasts a year longer. We've bought both, the XS won.

Funny experiment for anyone bored: I clicked one random product, a kids rain shell I think, every day for a week to confuse the engine. It now sends me the most beautifully unhinged emails. Chaos has a price and the price is my inbox.

Petition to separate the two features. Restock alerts: beloved, necessary, keep. Behavioral guess-what-you-want emails: divisive at best. Bundling them under one notifications toggle is why half this forum is mad. Let us choose.

Court Skort sizing check: the waistband runs snug but the shorts underneath run roomy, so it depends which half of your body votes. I went true to size and the waistband won the argument. If you're between sizes and hate waistband pressure, go up.
Wide feet folks, Stride Socks in the bigger size band stretch fine but the toe seam sits weird if you're between bands. Size up at the boundary. Tiny detail, twenty mile difference.

Realized today I've watched this company for a decade the way you watch a friend get famous. Proud, mostly. Weird, sometimes. They don't text back as fast as they used to but the friendship's real. That's my contribution to the four hundredth one of these threads. would recommend

quick note - Yoga crossover question for the Flow people: does the studio crowd at the clubhouse classes skew beginner friendly? Runner with the flexibility of a parking meter asking for a friend. The friend is me. The parking meter is also me.
the price drop alert on the thing i looked at arrived during my work presentation, lock screen, full product photo. my boss saw the legging. the legging is now a topic at work. thanks relay.
